query: US Iran negotiations Strait of Hormuz ceasefire agreement June 2026 latest
answer:

---

# US-Iran Negotiations, Strait of Hormuz & Ceasefire: Comprehensive Research Report (as of June 15, 2026)

## Executive Summary

The United States and Iran have been engaged in an active military conflict since **February 28, 2026**, when the US and Israel launched "Operation Midnight Hammer," which assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and destroyed Iran's key nuclear facilities. A fragile, informal ceasefire brokered by Pakistan took effect on **April 8, 2026**, but has been repeatedly violated by all parties. The **Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed to commercial shipping** since the war began — described as "the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy." **No formal peace agreement or binding ceasefire framework has been signed.** As of late May 2026, Qatar joined Pakistan as a co-mediator, and a 14-point memo on war-ending parameters was being negotiated, but Trump's five preconditions and Iran's counter-demands remain far apart.

---

## 1. War Background & Timeline

- **February 28, 2026**: US-Israel launched "Operation Midnight Hammer" — large-scale strikes on Iran that **assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei** (along with family members) and destroyed nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.
- **March 6**: Trump posted: "There will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER!"
- **March 8-9**: **Mojtaba Khamenei** (son of the deceased Supreme Leader) selected as new Supreme Leader by the Assembly of Experts.
- **Over 11,000 targets** struck across Iran; **3,000+ killed** by US-Israeli strikes.
- DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified (March 18) that **Iran's nuclear enrichment capabilities were destroyed**.
- This war was preceded by the **"Twelve-Day War" (June 13-24, 2025)** and five failed rounds of nuclear negotiations in 2025, plus four more failed rounds in early 2026.

## 2. Ceasefire Status

### The April 8 Ceasefire
- Brokered by **Pakistan** (PM Shehbaz Sharif and Field Marshal Asim Munir) based on a 10-point Iranian proposal.
- Initially declared as a **two-week ceasefire**; repeatedly extended by Trump unilaterally.
- **No signed document or binding framework** — entirely informal.

### Violations
The ceasefire has been violated extensively by all parties:
- **April 13**: US imposed a **naval blockade** of Iranian ports (51+ vessels redirected).
- **May 4-5**: Iran attacked UAE oil facilities at Fujairah (12 ballistic missiles, 3 cruise missiles, 4 drones); US claimed to sink 7 IRGC fast boats.
- **May 7**: US struck Iranian military sites in Bandar Abbas and Qeshm Island.
- **UAE** secretly struck Iran (including Lavan Island refinery).

### Status as of Late May 2026
- Described as **"on the brink" of collapse**; some analysts say it "effectively collapsed" when the US imposed the blockade on April 13.
- Pentagon chief stated the ceasefire is "not over" despite Hormuz tensions.
- **May 18**: Trump **postponed a scheduled attack** on Iran at the request of Qatar, Saudi Arabia, and UAE.
- **May 21**: IRGC threatened a **"global offensive"** if attacks restart.

## 3. Negotiations

### The Islamabad Talks (April 11-12, 2026)
- **First direct US-Iran talks since 1979**.
- ~300 US delegates (led by VP **JD Vance**) vs. ~70 Iranian delegates (led by parliament speaker **Ghalibaf**).
- 21-hour talks **ended without agreement**. Vance: "Iran refused to accept our terms."

### Subsequent Negotiation Attempts
- **May 2**: Iran submitted a **14-point ceasefire proposal** demanding a 30-day resolution timeline, full US withdrawal, end of blockade, sanctions lifted, and reparations. Trump called it "unacceptable."
- **May 7**: A **14-point memo** on war-ending parameters was being formulated via Pakistani mediators, calling on Iran to commit to a moratorium on nuclear enrichment.
- **May 17**: Trump set **five preconditions**: (1) Iran deliver 400 kg of enriched uranium; (2) maintain only one nuclear facility; (3) negotiations linked to cessation of hostilities; (4) refuse to release 25% of Iran's frozen assets; (5) reject Iran's reparations demands.
- **May 22**: **Qatar assumed a co-mediator role** alongside Pakistan. Trump said Iran is "dying to make a deal." CBS News reported the US was readying new strikes.

### Key Diplomatic Players
| Side | Key Figures |
|------|-------------|
| **US** | VP JD Vance, Steve Witkoff, Jared Kushner, Admiral Brad Cooper |
| **Iran** | FM Abbas Araghchi, Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf, New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei |
| **Mediators** | Pakistan (primary), Qatar (from May 22), Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt |

## 4. Strait of Hormuz

### Closure
- **Effectively closed to commercial shipping since February 28, 2026**.
- S&P Global described it as **"the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy."**
- Normally carries ~25% of world seaborne oil and ~20% of global LNG.
- Tanker traffic dropped **~70% immediately**, then to essentially zero.
- **~2,000 ships and ~20,000 mariners from 87 countries** stranded.
- Iran's IRGC warned ships entering without IRGC coordination "will be stopped by force."

### US "Project Freedom" (May 4, 2026)
- Trump announced a US naval escort operation to guide stranded ships through Hormuz.
- Supported by 3 Carrier Strike Groups, 2 Marine Expeditionary Units, hundreds of combat aircraft.
- **Maersk** confirmed one US-flagged vessel successfully exited, but ship-tracking data shows traffic remains **largely suspended**.

### US Naval Blockade of Iran (Since April 13)
- 51+ commercial vessels redirected.
- Iranian container ship **MV Touska** seized April 20; crew repatriated via Pakistan on May 4.
- Iran estimated to lose **$435 million per day** from the blockade.

## 5. Economic Impact

| Metric | Value | Date |
|--------|-------|------|
| US gasoline (pre-war) | $2.98/gal | Feb 26, 2026 |
| US gasoline (latest) | $4.45/gal (+49%) | May 3, 2026 |
| Brent crude (pre-war) | ~$72.50/bbl | Feb 27, 2026 |
| Brent crude (peak) | >$125/bbl | Late Apr 2026 |
| Brent crude (latest) | ~$110/bbl | May 5, 2026 |
| Global oil supply loss | ~2 million bpd | May 2026 |
| US war cost | ~$25 billion | Late Apr 2026 |
| Iran economic damage | $300B–$1T (Iran estimate) | Apr 2026 |
| Iran daily blockade loss | $435 million/day | Apr 2026 |
| US SPR releases | 17.5 million barrels | Mar-Apr 2026 |
| Trump approval | Below 30% | May 2026 |
| Goldman Sachs recession probability | 30% (12-month) | May 2026 |
| Q1 2026 PCE inflation | +4.5% annualized | Q1 2026 |

## 6. Iran Domestic Situation
- **Mojtaba Khamenei** is the new Supreme Leader but his authority is not consolidated.
- President **Pezeshkian** branded IRGC escalation as "madness," revealing severe civilian-military rifts.
- Near-total **internet shutdown** for 1,550+ hours affecting 90 million Iranians.
- Economy in severe crisis with mass layoffs and inflation.

## 7. Data Gaps & Caveats

**Critical gap**: The most detailed Iran-specific data runs through **May 23, 2026**, with some market context from June 5. Events from **May 24 through June 15, 2026** are not covered in the available sources. Key unknowns include:
1. Whether the ceasefire has held, collapsed, or been replaced
2. Whether the US resumed strikes after the May 18 postponement
3. Progress on the 14-point memo and Qatar/Pakistan mediation
4. Whether "Project Freedom" achieved sustained commercial shipping through Hormuz
5. Current oil prices and Hormuz shipping status in June 2026

---

**Sources consulted**: Reuters, AP News, Al Jazeera, BBC News, CNBC, NPR, Washington Post, NBC News, Axios, CBS News, Time, Fox News, Iran International, CSIS, Wikipedia ("2026 Iran war," "2025-2026 Iran-United States negotiations," "2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis," "Islamabad Talks"), S&P Global, IEA, EIA, AAA Gas Prices, Goldman Sachs, Moody's Analytics, FRED.

key_facts: The US and Iran have been in active military conflict since February 28, 2026 ('Operation Midnight Hammer'), when US-Israeli strikes assassinated Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and destroyed Iran's nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.
A fragile informal ceasefire brokered by Pakistan took effect on April 8, 2026, but has been repeatedly violated by all parties and has no signed document or binding framework.

The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed to commercial shipping since February 28, 2026 — described by S&P Global as 'the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy.' Approximately 2,000 ships and 20,000 mariners from 87 countries are stranded.

The Islamabad Talks (April 11-12, 2026) — the first direct US-Iran talks since 1979 — ended without agreement after 21 hours. VP JD Vance led ~300 US delegates; Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf led ~70 Iranian delegates.

Trump set five preconditions on May 17, 2026: Iran deliver 400 kg of enriched uranium, maintain only one nuclear facility, link negotiations to cessation of hostilities, refuse to release 25% of Iran's frozen assets, and reject reparations demands.
Iran submitted a 14-point ceasefire proposal on May 2 demanding full US withdrawal, end of blockade, sanctions lifted, and reparations — rejected by Trump as 'unacceptable.'
A 14-point memo on war-ending parameters was being negotiated via Pakistani mediators as of May 7, calling on Iran to commit to a moratorium on nuclear enrichment.
Qatar assumed a co-mediator role alongside Pakistan on May 22, 2026. Saudi Arabia and UAE also requested Trump postpone a scheduled attack on Iran on May 18.
The US imposed a naval blockade of Iranian ports since April 13, redirecting 51+ commercial vessels. Iran estimated losses of $435 million/day.
Trump launched 'Project Freedom' on May 4 — a US naval escort operation through the Strait of Hormuz — but ship-tracking data shows commercial traffic remains largely suspended.

Mojtaba Khamenei (son of assassinated Supreme Leader) was selected as new Supreme Leader on March 8-9, but his authority is not consolidated. President Pezeshkian branded IRGC escalation as 'madness.'
DNI Tulsi Gabbard testified on March 18 that Iran's nuclear enrichment capabilities were destroyed by the US-Israeli strikes.
The IRGC threatened a 'global offensive' on May 21 if attacks restart. CBS News reported on May 22 that the US was readying new strikes on Iran.
No formal peace agreement or binding ceasefire framework has been signed as of the latest available data (late May 2026). The situation remains highly volatile.
Data gap: Detailed Iran-specific coverage runs through May 23, 2026. Events from May 24 through June 15, 2026 are not covered in available sources.
